frame_analyses.description: The system interface is displayed, ready for procedure selection.
The needle holder is visible with the needle about to be loaded.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The user begins to position the needle for loading.

frame_analyses.description: Suture is being placed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Placement appears accurate with minimal deviation.

frame_analyses.description: The first throw, a surgeon's knot, is being tied.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: The knot technique appears secure with a clear double throw.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw is being tied, hand appears to switch from the first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching observed, throw technique acceptable.

frame_analyses.description: The user is tying the third throw, switching hands again.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switch is conducted, knot appears stable.

frame_analyses.description: Both ends of the suture are being cut inside the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut placement appears inside, completing the procedure.

frame_analyses.description: The system interface returns, indicating completion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Procedure seems completed, no further actions required.

knot_assessments.notes: Single throw with hand switch executed cleanly.
Final throw secured, proper hand switch observed.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit consistently closed with no visible gaps.

technique_summary: The procedure was completed with secure knot ties and proper hand switching. Suture placement was slightly off but within acceptable limits.
improvement_suggestions: Continue to practice smooth hand switching technique.
